title: Update your widget to be compatible with Interface Version 2
breadcrumbs: Update your widget to be compatible with Interface Version 2
text: Update your widget to be compatible with Interface Version 2 To make your code compatible with the new interface version 2 with minimum effort, you can use the compatibility utility. It is located by default in the uniface/webapps/ux_compatibility_utils/v2_to_v1_ux_interface_converter.js. folder. The compatibility utility consists of a set of JavaScript modules that facilitate the conversion of UX Widget API data arguments into the new format required by Interface Version 2. For more information, see Compatibility Utility . For detailed usage with examples, see How to Use the Compatibility Utility . The following Utility Functions are available: dataUpdate_data(data) Converts the data argument of the dataUpdate() function to the old format. dataCleanup_dataNames(dataNames) Converts the dataNames argument of the dataCleanup() function to the old format. processLayout_objectDefinition(objectDefinition) Converts an array of property names from the objectDefinition argument of the processLayout() function to the old format. onConnect_objectDefinition(objectDefinition) Converts an array of property names from the objectDefinition argument of the onConnect() function to the old format. Module Location & Import File Path : uniface/webapps/ux_compatibility_utils/v2_to_v1_ux_interface_converter.js Usage : import { v2_to_v1 } from '../../ux_compatibility_utils/v2_to_v1_ux_interface_converter.js'; Utility Function Overview with Examples dataUpdate_data(data) This function transforms a Version 2-style data object , where all properties are defined as independent key-value pairs using prefixed strings (e.g., "html:disabled", "style:padding"), into a Version 1-style grouped object , where properties are nested under categories like html, style, or class. V2 Format V1 Format (via Compatibility) "html:disabled": true html: { disabled: true } "style:padding": "8px" style: { padding: "8px" } Explain this code import { v2_to_v1 } from '../../ux_compatibility_utils/v2_to_v1_ux_interface_converter.js'; dataUpdate(data) { // Convert the V2 flat data format to the V1 grouped format const data = v2_to_v1.dataUpdate_data(data); // Access properties as grouped objects (same as V1 expectations) if (data.html?.disabled) { // Apply the disabled attribute if defined in the HTML section this.inputElement.disabled = true; } if (data.style?.padding) { // Apply padding from the style group this.inputElement.style.padding = data.style.padding; } if (data.class?.outline) { // Add a class if defined in the class group this.inputElement.classList.add("outline"); } if (data.value) { // Handle field value if available this.inputElement.value = data.value; } Copy dataCleanup_dataNames(dataNames) This function converts a Version 2-style dataNames Set , which contains a flat list of prefixed strings (e.g., "html:disabled", "style:padding"), into a Version 1-style object where each property type (html, style, class) maps to a Set of property names belonging to that type. V2 Format V1 Format (via Compatibility) Set(["html:disabled", "style:padding"]) { html: Set("disabled"), style: Set("padding") } Explain this code import { v2_to_v1 } from '../../ux_compatibility_utils/v2_to_v1_ux_interface_converter.js'; dataCleanup(dataNames) { // Convert the V2 flat Set of keys into V1-style grouped Sets const dataNames = v2_to_v1.dataCleanup_dataNames(dataNames); // Clean up HTML-related properties if (dataNames.html?.has("disabled")) { this.inputElement.removeAttribute("disabled"); } // Clean up style-related properties if (dataNames.style?.has("padding")) { this.inputElement.style.padding = ""; } // Clean up class-related properties if (dataNames.class?.has("outline")) { this.inputElement.classList.remove("outline"); } } Copy processLayout_objectDefinition(objectDefinition) This function wraps the original objectDefinition object and overrides its getPropertyNames() method. It ensures that any uncategorized property (i.e., without known prefixes like html:, style:, class:) is returned with a uniface: prefix — restoring the behavior expected in Interface V1. V2 Property Name V1 Property Name "html:disabled" "html:disabled" (unchanged) "label-text" "uniface:label-text" Explain this code import { v2_to_v1 } from '../../ux_compatibility_utils/v2_to_v1_ux_interface_converter.js'; processLayout(skeletonWidgetElement, objectDefinition) { const objectDefinition = v2_to_v1.processLayout_objectDefinition(objectDefinition); // Now calling getPropertyNames() returns the modified list const propertyNames = objectDefinition.getPropertyNames(); if (propertyNames.includes("uniface:label-text")) { // Access uncategorized property in its original name const label = objectDefinition.getProperty("uniface:label-text"); this.label.textContent = label; } } Copy onConnect_objectDefinition(objectDefinition) This function is functionally identical to processLayout_objectDefinition() . Explain this code import { v2_to_v1 } from '../../ux_compatibility_utils/v2_to_v1_ux_interface_converter.js'; onConnect(WidgetElement, objectDefinition) { const objectDefinition = v2_to_v1.onConnect_objectDefinition(objectDefinition); // Now calling getPropertyNames() returns the modified list const propertyNames = objectDefinition.getPropertyNames(); if (propertyNames.includes("uniface:label-text")) { // Access uncategorized property in its original name const label = objectDefinition.getProperty("uniface:label-text"); this.label.textContent = label; } } Copy After updating your widget, set uxInterfaceVersion = 2 in the physical widget definition section of the web.ini file. This indicates that the widget is now compatible with Interface Version 2. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
